tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows impaired renal function (elevated creatinine), proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, ketones), suggesting possible renal disease or nephrotic syndrome. Dyslipidemia is present (high total and LDL cholesterol, low HDL). HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite normal glucose, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of possible underlying causes.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
